zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Robert Bindloss於1530年出生在英國藍卡爾波維其可 霍爾時，他的父親Sir William Byndlose30歲，母親Eleanor Berners Palmer26歲。他和Lady Agnes Harrison至少有6個兒子和3個女兒。他去世於1595年在他的家鄉享年65歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir Robert Bindloss于1530年出生在英国藍卡爾波維其可 霍爾时，他的父亲Sir William Byndlose30岁，母亲Eleanor Berners Palmer26岁。他和Lady Agnes Harrison至少有6个儿子和3个女儿。他去世于1595年在他的家乡享年65岁。

nameMeanings.name: Bindloss
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: English: nickname, occupational name perhaps for a wolf hunter, from Middle English binden ‘bind’ + Middle English (Old French) loues ‘wolves’. Plurals are most unusual in phrasal nicknames. The more regular singular form occurs in the name of John Byndeloue, 1327 in Subsidy Rolls (Suffolk), for which see Bensley , and is synonymous with Truslove ( see Trussler ). The preposition in the 1302–3 example could alternatively point to a lost place-name but it is more likely to be a scribal error. The earliest examples are from the NR Yorks and WR Yorks border (Ripon, Sharow, and Langthorpe are almost neighbouring settlements), and the surname persisted in NR Yorks until the late 17th century, but its subsequent history belongs mainly to NW England, where it first appears in the previous century.

variants.name: Bendelow
meanings: English: reduced form of Bendelowes, a variant of Bindloss .

variants.name: Bensley
meanings: English (Norfolk and Suffolk): possibly an altered form of Bendelowes with metathesis of -s- and substitution of -ley for -low). Bendelowes is a variant of Bindloss, a nickname perhaps for a wolf hunter, from Middle English binden ‘bind’ + Middle English (Old French) loues ‘wolves’.

variants.name: Subsidy Rolls
meanings: 1 English: nickname from Middle English god(e), gud(e), goud(e) ‘good’ + inough ‘abundant; sufficient, adequate, satisfactory’. Compare Robert Welynogh, 1260 in Assize Rolls (Cambs), William Welynough (Welynou), 1327 in Subsidy Rolls (Essex). Some examples may belong under (2).
2 English: nickname from Old English gōd ‘good’ + cnafa ‘boy, servant’.

variants.name: Binsley
meanings: English: (i) variant of Bindloss with metathesis of -s- and substitution of -ley for -low. Redmonds, Dictionary of Yorks Surnames records John Binlus alias Binsley, 1655 in Inquisitiones post Mortem (Bedale, NR Yorks), and William Benlowes, Binslow, or Billoes, 1628–49 in Parish Registers (Pickhill, NR Yorks). Tooth (3, p. 37) notes the currency of Bindloes and Bindloss in modern Staffs, where Binnesley also occurs. Compare Bensley . (ii) the Staffs name may alternatively be a variant of Binley with an intrusive -s-.

variants.name: Trussler
meanings: English (Surrey and Sussex; of Norman origin): nickname or occupational name, from Old French trousser ‘to truss, bind, carry off’ + love ‘(she-)wolf’, probably for a wolf-hunter. Compare Manlove .

nameMeanings.name: Robert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the many French names of Germanic origin that were introduced into Britain by the Normans; it has since remained in continuous use. It is derived from the nearly synonymous elements hrōd ‘fame’ + berht ‘bright, famous’, and had a native Old English predecessor of similar form (Hreodbeorht), which was supplanted by the Norman name. Two dukes of Normandy in the 11th century bore the name: the father of William the Conqueror (sometimes identified with the legendary Robert the Devil), and his eldest son. It was borne also by three kings of Scotland, notably Robert the Bruce ( 1274–1329 ), who freed Scotland from English domination. The altered short form Bob is very common, but Hob and Dob, which were common in the Middle Ages and gave rise to surnames, are extinct. See also Rupert .

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Robert Bindloss was born in 1530, in Borwick Hall, Lancashire, England, his father, Sir William Byndlose, was 30 and his mother, Eleanor Berners Palmer, was 26. He had at least 6 sons and 3 daughters with Lady Agnes Harrison. He died in 1595, in his hometown, at the age of 65.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir Robert Bindloss est né en 1530, à Borwick Hall, Lancashire, Angleterre, son père, Sir William Byndlose, avait 30 ans et sa mère, Eleanor Berners Palmer, avait 26 ans. Il a eu au moins 6 fils et 3 filles avec Lady Agnes Harrison. Il est décédé en 1595, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 65 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir Robert Bindloss 1530 in Borwick Hall, Lancashire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir William Byndlose 30 und seine Mutter Eleanor Berners Palmer 26. Er hatte mit Lady Agnes Harrison mindestens 6 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Er starb 1595 im Alter von 65 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Robert Bindloss è nato il nel 1530 nel Borwick Hall, Lancashire, England, il suo padre, Sir William Byndlose, aveva 30 anni e il suo madre, Eleanor Berners Palmer, aveva 26 anni. Lui avuto almeno 6 figli e 3 figlie con Lady Agnes Harrison. Morì nel 1595, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 65 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir Robert Bindlossが1530年にイギリスランカシュアイアボルウィック ハルで生まれた時，父Sir William Byndloseは30歳，母Eleanor Berners Palmerは26歳であった。彼はLady Agnes Harrisonとの間に少なくとも6人の息子と3人の娘をもうけた。彼は1595年に故郷で65歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Robert Bindloss(이)가 1530년 영국 랭카실르 볼윅 홀에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir William Byndlose(은)는 30세였고, 그의 어머니 Eleanor Berners Palmer(은)는 26세였다. 그는 Lady Agnes Harrison(와)과 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1595년 그의 고향에서 65세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Robert Bindloss nasceu em 1530, em Borwick Hall, Lancashire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir William Byndlose, tinha 30 anos e sua mãe, Eleanor Berners Palmer, tinha 26 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 6 filhos e 3 filhas com Lady Agnes Harrison. Ele faleceu em 1595, em sua cidade natal, com 65 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir Robert Bindloss родился в 1530 г. в местности борвикк холл, ланкашир, Англия, его отцу по имени Sir William Byndlose было 30 лет, а матери, которую звали Eleanor Berners Palmer, 26. У него было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 3 дочери от Lady Agnes Harrison. Он умер в 1595 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 65 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir Robert Bindloss nació en 1530, en Borwick Hall, Lancashire, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir William Byndlose, tenía 30 años y su mamá, Eleanor Berners Palmer, tenía 26 años. Tuvo por lo menos 6 hijos y 3 hijas con Lady Agnes Harrison. Murió en 1595, en su pueblo, a la edad de 65 años.
